architecture.summary: DeepSym trains a predictive deep encoder-decoder network on robot interaction data: the encoder compresses images and actions into a binary bottleneck layer representing action-grounded object categories; the decoder reconstructs scene effects in pixel coordinates. After training, a decision tree distills the network's decoder function, and decision paths are converted into probabilistic rules in PPDDL format for symbolic planning. The system bridges sensorimotor experience (raw images, actions, effects) to discrete symbolic representations and executable plans.

biblio.title: DeepSym: Deep Symbol Generation and Rule Learning for Planning from Unsupervised Robot Interaction

claims.statement: The binary latent vector represents action-driven object categories based on the interaction experience of the robot
The system discovers discrete representations of object properties such as 'rollable' and 'insertable'

claims.dataset: simulated robotic manipulator multi-step object manipulation
claims.statement: Learned symbols allow for the generation of effective plans for achieving goals, such as building towers of the desired height
The system is applicable to the MNIST 8-puzzle domain in which learned symbols allow for the generation of plans that move the empty tile into any given position

Probabilistic rules are extracted from decision tree paths and represented in Probabilistic Planning Domain Definition Language (PPDDL), allowing off-the-shelf planners to operate on the knowledge extracted from sensorimotor experience
